paper_title: Study on Mechanical and Viscoelastic Properties of Asphalt Mixture Modified by Diatomite and Crumb Rubber Particles

methods.description: This method involves analyzing the relaxation properties of asphalt mixtures using the second-order extensive Maxwell model and the Scott-Blair model. The goal is to understand how different modifications, such as the addition of rubber particles and diatomite, affect the relaxation modulus and stress relaxation ability of asphalt mixtures.

steps.description: Apply the second-order extensive Maxwell model and the Scott-Blair model to analyze the relaxation moduli E(t) of the eight types of asphalt mixtures with time.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Maxwell and Scott-Blair models to analyze relaxation moduli of asphalt mixtures

steps.description: Solve the unknown parameters of Equations (16) and (17) using EXCEL software.
Compare the fitting curves of the Scott-Blair model and second-order extended Maxwell model with the calculated results to validate the models.
steps.searchable_summary: validate model fitting with calculated results

steps.description: Analyze the effect of rubber particles and diatomite on the relaxation modulus and stress relaxation ability of asphalt mixtures.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze effect of additives on relaxation modulus

steps.description: Compare the test results of different modified asphalt to determine the best relaxation characteristics.
steps.searchable_summary: compare test results of modified asphalt
